$meta.usedBy: Feature C1 customer upsert — dedup lookup by external_reference

cursor.description: Opaque cursor from previous response.next_cursor; omit for the first request.

filter.description: URL-encoded JSON array of filter objects: [{field, operator, value}]. Supported filterable fields and their operators: id (lt/lteq/gt/gteq/eq/not_eq/in/not_in), customer_type (eq/not_eq), ledger_account_id (eq/not_eq), name (start_with/eq), external_reference (start_with/eq/not_eq/in/not_in), reg_no (eq/not_eq/in/not_in), emails (in).

sort.description: Field name, prefix with `-` for descending.

has_more.description: Indicates whether additional results are available beyond this set.

next_cursor.description: Cursor to retrieve the next set of results. `null` when no further results.

items.description: oneOf: Company customer (has `name`, `reg_no`, `vat_number`, `customer_type: "company"`) or Individual customer (has `first_name`, `last_name`, `customer_type: "individual"`). Both variants include `id`, `customer_type`, `external_reference`, `billing_address`, etc.

notes: Response list key is `items`, NOT `customers`. Earlier fixture versions mistakenly documented `{customers: [...], metadata: {cursor, limit}}` — that shape is fabricated. Verified against the real spec on 2026-04-13.
